topics.title: Next.js 16.3 Deep Dive: 90% Less Turbopack Memory and 5.5x Faster Builds
topics.whyTrending: Next.js 16.3 (June 29, 2026) shipped on-disk cache eviction cutting dev-server memory by up to 90%, persisted production build cache (5.5x faster), an experimental Rust port of the React Compiler, and Vite-compatible import.meta.glob. Fresh release, high search volume, real migration questions.

topics.title: CSS Grid Lanes: Native Masonry Is Finally Here (After Years of Browser Wars)
topics.whyTrending: CSS Working Group settled the years-long masonry debate on display: grid-lanes. Pinterest-style layouts without JavaScript are now possible natively. Interop 2026 topic with strong SEO potential and great demo/code opportunities.

topics.title: TanStack Start vs Next.js in 2026: I Built the Same App in Both
topics.whyTrending: TanStack Start is the loudest 'Next.js killer' conversation of 2026 — type-safe routing, Vite speed, Nitro deploy portability vs RSC-native Next.js. Comparison content ranks well and sparks discussion.

topics.title: React Compiler in Production: Is Manual useMemo/useCallback Officially Legacy?
topics.whyTrending: After React Compiler v1.0 (Oct 2025), 2026 adoption makes manual memoization look legacy. Next.js 16.3 even ships an experimental Rust port. Practical 'what to delete from your codebase' angle with before/after benchmarks.

topics.title: CSS Anchor Positioning: Tooltips and Dropdowns Without a Single Line of JS
topics.whyTrending: Anchor positioning is the hot 'delete your JavaScript' CSS feature of 2026, but browser support is still uneven — a practical guide with fallback strategies fills a real gap. Pairs with Popover API.

topics.title: Server-First Won: How RSC and Cache Components Changed Frontend Architecture
topics.whyTrending: The pendulum swung back to server-first in 2026 — RSC everywhere, Next.js Cache Components with Partial Pre-Rendering, less client JS. Architecture think-piece with practical migration guidance.

topics.title: INP in 2026: The Core Web Vital That's Quietly Killing Your Rankings
topics.whyTrending: Interaction to Next Paint replaced FID and is now the metric separating well-architected products from struggling ones. Practical debugging guide (long tasks, hydration, third-party scripts) has strong evergreen SEO.

topics.title: Is AI Causing Frontend's Second Lost Decade? A Hacker News Debate, Dissected
topics.whyTrending: June 2026 HN thread on AI's impact on frontend quality went viral. Opinion piece with data (68% of devs use AI codegen) — controversy drives shares, and it fits the blog's AI + frontend expertise overlap.

topics.title: The 2026 React Stack: oRPC, Drizzle, Better-Auth, TanStack — What Tutorials Don't Cover
topics.whyTrending: A May 2026 HN thread crystallized 'the modern React stack' (Next.js + oRPC + Zod + TanStack Query/Form + Drizzle + Better-Auth + shadcn). Stack breakdowns rank well and internal-link into many existing posts.

topics.title: Container Scroll-State Queries: Style Sticky Headers Without JavaScript Observers
topics.whyTrending: Container queries evolved — scroll-state() lets you style stuck headers, snapped items, and scrollable overflow states in pure CSS. New in 2026, low competition, very demo-friendly.
